name: Missile Command
description: The player defends six cities from a barrage of incoming ballistic missiles. The missiles originate from multiple points in the sky and descend towards the cities and the player's missile batteries. The goal is to protect the cities from destruction for as long as possible. The player controls three missile batteries located at the bottom of the screen: one on the left, one in the center, and one on the right. Each battery has a finite supply of defensive missiles. The player selects a battery and then designates a point in the sky where the defensive missile will detonate. Upon detonation, the defensive missile creates an explosion that destroys any enemy missiles that are within its radius. The game ends when all six cities are destroyed. After certain score thresholds, the player is awarded bonus cities. After each wave, the missile batteries are restocked with missiles, but the number of missiles available will be decreased in later rounds, or can be limited by the game settings. Enemy missiles may also include smart bombs that split into multiple missiles upon detonation.

controls.q: Move the target cursor to the upper left quadrant of the screen.
controls.w: Move the target cursor to the upper center quadrant of the screen.
controls.e: Move the target cursor to the upper right quadrant of the screen.
controls.a: Move the target cursor to the center left quadrant of the screen.
controls.s: Move the target cursor to the exact center of the screen.
controls.d: Move the target cursor to the center right quadrant of the screen.
controls.z: Move the target cursor to the lower left quadrant of the screen.
controls.x: Move the target cursor to the lower center quadrant of the screen.
controls.c: Move the target cursor to the lower right quadrant of the screen.
controls.space: Launch a missile from the currently selected battery to detonate at the target cursor's location.
